'''Scott Roskell''' (born 20 April 1969) is an Australian former professional [[rugby union]] and [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played rugby union for [[London Welsh]], and rugby league for the [[London Broncos|London Crusaders]], as a {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-roundup-nolans-in-tune-as-hull-prevail-1467239.html|title = Rugby League Round-Up: Nolans in tune as Hull prevail|website = [[Independent.co.uk]]|date = 13 December 1993}}</ref>

Roskell joined [[London Broncos#1991–1994: London Crusaders|London Crusaders]] in August 1992.<ref name="halloffame">{{cite web|title=London Broncos Supporters Association Hall of Fame launched|url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/5360.php|publisher=London Broncos Rugby League|accessdate=14 October 2014|date=5 July 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20141019085205/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/5360.php|archivedate=19 October 2014|df=dmy-all}}</ref> The club was later renamed to London Broncos, and Roskell went on to play for the club in the first two seasons of the [[Super League]]. He left the club in 1997, and switched to rugby union to play for [[London Welsh]].

Roskell scored 86 tries for the Broncos – a club record until it was broken in 2012 by [[Luke Dorn]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/982.php |title=Match Centre | Fixtures & Results | Rugby | London Broncos Rugby League | Official Site : Harlequins RL 32 Leeds Rhinos 22 |access-date=23 October 2012 |archive-date=19 October 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141019104812/http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/rugby/fixturesandresults/982.php |url-status=dead }}</ref>

In 2014, Roskell was an inaugural inductee to the London Broncos' Hall of Fame.<ref name="halloffame" />
